From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

5-star hotel in Donetsk, Ukraine

**Donbass Palace,** or **Donbas Palace,** is the first 5-star hotel in [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk"), [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"). It is located along [Artema St, 80](https://uk.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D0%92%D1%83%D0%BB%D0%B8%D1%86%D1%8F_%D0%90%D1%80%D1%82%D0%B5%D0%BC%D0%B0_(%D0%94%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B5%D1%86%D1%8C%D0%BA) "uk:Вулиця Артема (Донецьк)"), Donetsk in Donetsk Oblast.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/1938._%D0%9E%D0%BA%D0%BE%D0%BD%D1%87%D0%B0%D0%BD%D0%B8%D0%B5_%D1%81%D1%82%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B8%D1%82%D0%B5%D0%BB%D1%8C%D1%81%D1%82%D0%B2%D0%B0_%D0%B3%D0%BE%D1%81%D1%82%D0%B8%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D1%8B_%D0%94%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B1%D0%B0%D1%81%D1%81.jpg/250px-1938._%D0%9E%D0%BA%D0%BE%D0%BD%D1%87%D0%B0%D0%BD%D0%B8%D0%B5_%D1%81%D1%82%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B8%D1%82%D0%B5%D0%BB%D1%8C%D1%81%D1%82%D0%B2%D0%B0_%D0%B3%D0%BE%D1%81%D1%82%D0%B8%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D1%8B_%D0%94%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B1%D0%B0%D1%81%D1%81.jpg)](/wiki/File:1938._%D0%9E%D0%BA%D0%BE%D0%BD%D1%87%D0%B0%D0%BD%D0%B8%D0%B5_%D1%81%D1%82%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B8%D1%82%D0%B5%D0%BB%D1%8C%D1%81%D1%82%D0%B2%D0%B0_%D0%B3%D0%BE%D1%81%D1%82%D0%B8%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%86%D1%8B_%D0%94%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B1%D0%B0%D1%81%D1%81.jpg)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donbass_Palace&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Hotel "Donbass" was built in 1938 upon the plans of N. Rechanikov and A. Shuvalova. During the construction, the project was redesigned by N. Porkhunov.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Panteley Tamurov supervised construction. During the [Nazi occupation](/wiki/Nazi_occupation "Nazi occupation") of [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk"), the [Gestapo](/wiki/Gestapo "Gestapo") was headquartered in the hotel; the building was partially destroyed during the war.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

From 1947 to 1949, the building was renovated, but the [dome](/wiki/Dome "Dome") was not restored due to a lack of funds. In 1971, the [Donbas National Academy of Civil Engineering and Architecture](/wiki/Donbas_National_Academy_of_Civil_Engineering_and_Architecture "Donbas National Academy of Civil Engineering and Architecture") conducted restoration works.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

The hotel was considered an architectural monument of the Donetsk region. On 26 February 2001, the old building was blown up. On 15 May 2004, the [five star hotel](/wiki/Five_star_hotel "Five star hotel") "Donbas Palace" was opened. The new building differs slightly from the original. Currently, Donbas Palace is owned by [ESTA Group](/wiki/SCM_Holdings#ESTA_Holding_-_Real_Estate "SCM Holdings"), which operates the real estate business of [System Capital Management](/wiki/System_Capital_Management "System Capital Management") group.

During the [cornerstone laying](/wiki/Cornerstone "Cornerstone") in October 2001, [Rinat Akhmetov](/wiki/Rinat_Akhmetov "Rinat Akhmetov") buried a [time capsule](/wiki/Time_capsule "Time capsule").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 2008, Donbass Palace served as the backdrop for the screen adaptation of Dmitry Gerasimov's novel "Cross in the Circle"[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In 2009, Donetsk journalist Yevgeniy Yasenov published a book named *Donbass Palace* [[10]](#cite_note-10) dedicated to the 5-year anniversary of the hotel.

## Facilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donbass_Palace&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Facilities")]

Donbas Palace is located in the centre of [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk") near the [Drama Theatre](/wiki/Donetsk_Regional_Russian_Drama_Theatre "Donetsk Regional Russian Drama Theatre"), the [Opera House](/wiki/Donetsk#Donetsk_Opera_and_Ballet_Theatre "Donetsk") and commercial areas. The hotel offers 129 rooms,[[11]](#cite_note-11) an indoor [pool](/wiki/Swimming_pool "Swimming pool"), [spa-center](/wiki/Spa "Spa"), and [fitness club](/wiki/Fitness_club "Fitness club"). Donbas Palace Hotel has two [a la carte](/wiki/A_la_carte "A la carte") restaurants, a lobby lounge and L'Opera Hall which serves as a concert hall or a ball room. For business meetings, the hotel provides a business centre and two conference halls.

## Awards and recognition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Donbass_Palace&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards and recognition")]

Donbas Palace is the first Ukrainian hotel to become a member of the [LHW](/wiki/Leading_Hotels_of_the_World "Leading Hotels of the World")[[12]](#cite_note-12)

13 November 2005 - “Ukraine’s Leading Hotel 2005”  
20 September 2006 - “Ukraine’s Leading Hotel 2006” and “Ukraine’s Leading Business Hotel 2006”[[13]](#cite_note-13)
